No Poverty.reason: The course content and objectives do not provide any evidence related to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course content and objectives do not provide any evidence related to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content and objectives do not provide any evidence related to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a graduate-level seminar, directly providing quality tertiary education (Target 4.3). Its emphasis on comparative law and global vision also contributes to knowledge for sustainable development (Target 4.7).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course content and objectives do not provide any evidence related to decent work or economic growth.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course content and objectives do not provide any evidence related to industry, innovation, or infrastructure.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The study of constitutional law inherently involves principles of equal opportunity and non-discriminatory laws (Target 10.3). The course's core competency of 'social participation and humane care' further supports this link.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course directly studies constitutional law, frameworks, and judicial bodies (French Constitutional Council), which are fundamental to promoting the rule of law (Target 16.3) and building strong, accountable institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's emphasis on comparing constitutional systems across countries and fostering a 'global vision' directly supports international cooperation and multi-stakeholder partnerships for achieving global goals (Target 17.16).
